```
# Release Posts for Stationpedia Ascended v0.4.0

---

## Reddit Post (r/Stationeers)

### Title:
**[MOD] Stationpedia Ascended v0.4.0 - In-Game Notepad, Survival Manual, Video Support & More!**

### Body:

Hey Stationeers!

I'm excited to release **Stationpedia Ascended v0.4.0** - a major update that transforms Stationpedia into a comprehensive knowledge hub with an integrated note-taking system.

## 📝 Station Notepad (NEW!)
Take notes directly in-game! Press **F2** to open your personal notepad.

- **Folders & Organization** - Create folders to organize your notes
- **Global & Per-Save Notes** - Some notes follow you everywhere, others are save-specific
- **Rich Text Formatting** - Bold, italic, colors, headers, bullet points
- **Link to Stationpedia** - Type `{LINK:StructureArcFurnace;Arc Furnace}` to create clickable links that open Stationpedia pages!

## 📚 New Guides & Content

**Survival Manual** - A complete beginner's guide covering:
- First 5 minutes survival
- Suit management
- Building your first shelter
- Power systems basics
- Atmosphere control

**Game Mechanics** (with data from game code):
- **Complete Smelting Guide** - All furnace types, recipes, temperatures, alloys, Ice-Only recipes, Gaseous Fuel recipes
- **Species Survival Guide** - Human, Zrilian, and Robot requirements pulled directly from the source code

**Additional Guides:**
- Power Systems
- Airlock Systems (Basic & Advanced)
- Air Conditioning (including the "your starter kit is a bomb" warning!)
- Daylight Sensor

## 🎬 Media Support
- Embed **videos** directly in Stationpedia pages
- Inline **images** for visual guides
- Clickable **YouTube links**

## 🎨 Other Features
- **Nested Table of Contents** - Multi-level TOC with smooth scrolling
- **Vanilla/Ascended Mode** - Click the "Stationpedia" header to toggle styling (easter egg!)
- **Enhanced Tooltips** - Hover over logic types, slots, memory for detailed explanations

## Installation
Requires **BepInEx** with **StationeersLaunchPad** plugin.

**Steam Workshop:** https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3634225688

## Screenshots
[Add your screenshots here]

---

Let me know what guides you'd like to see next! I'm considering AIMEE, LARRE, and Stirling Engine guides.

Happy engineering! 🚀

---

## Discord Post (Stationeers Discord)

```
🎉 **Stationpedia Ascended v0.4.0 Released!**

Major update with **in-game note-taking** and tons of new content!

📝 **Station Notepad** (Press F2)
• Take notes in-game with folders & rich text
• Create clickable links to any Stationpedia page!
• Global notes persist across all saves

📚 **New Guides**
• Complete Survival Manual for beginners
• Smelting Guide with ALL recipes from game code
• Species Survival (Human/Zrilian/Robot requirements)
• Power, Airlock, Air Conditioning guides

🎬 **Media Support**
• Videos & images embedded in pages
• YouTube links

🔗 **Workshop:** https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3634225688

Requires BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad

What guides would you like next? 🤔
```

---

## Short Twitter/X Post

```
🚀 Stationpedia Ascended v0.4.0 for #Stationeers!

📝 In-game notepad (F2) with Stationpedia links
📚 Survival Manual & Smelting Guide
🎬 Video/image support in pages
🔧 Data from actual game code

Workshop: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3634225688
```

---

• Station Notepad - In-game notes with folders, rich text, Stationpedia links
• Survival Manual - Complete beginner's guide
• Smelting Guide - All recipes, furnaces, alloys from game code
• Species Survival - Human/Zrilian/Robot requirements
• Video & Image support in pages
• Nested Table of Contents
• Power, Airlock, Air Conditioning guides
```

- newString:

```

  <!-- Package mod to SLP mods folder for testing as released mod -->
  <Target Name="Package">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <ModsFolder>$(USERPROFILE)\Documents\My Games\Stationeers\mods\StationpediaAscended</ModsFolder>
      <LocalModsFolder>$(StationeersPath)\mods\StationpediaAscended</LocalModsFolder>
      <BuildOutputFolder>$(ProjectDir)build_release</BuildOutputFolder>
    </PropertyGroup>
    
    <!-- Create mod folder structure -->
    <MakeDir Directories="$(ModsFolder)" />
    <MakeDir Directories="$(ModsFolder)\About" />
    <MakeDir Directories="$(ModsFolder)\images" />
    <MakeDir Directories="$(LocalModsFolder)" />
    <MakeDir Directories="$(LocalModsFolder)\About" />
    <MakeDir Directories="$(LocalModsFolder)\images" />
    
    <!-- Get all image files -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <ImageFiles Include="$(ProjectDir)images\*.*" />
    </ItemGroup>
    
    <!-- Copy mod files to Documents mods folder -->
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(BuildOutputFolder)\StationpediaAscended.dll" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)descriptions.json" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)stationpediaascended_ui" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\About.xml" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)\About" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\Preview.png" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)\About" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="@(ImageFiles)" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)\images" />
    
    <!-- Also copy to Stationeers local mods folder for Workshop uploads -->
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(BuildOutputFolder)\StationpediaAscended.dll" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)descriptions.json" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)stationpediaascended_ui" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\About.xml" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)\About" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\Preview.png" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)\About" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="@(ImageFiles)" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)\images" />
    
    <Message Text="============================================" Importance="high" />
    <Message Text="Mod packaged to: $(ModsFolder)" Importance="high" />
    <Message Text="Mod packaged to: $(LocalModsFolder)" Importance="high" />
    <Message Text="Ready for testing and Workshop upload!" Importance="high" />
    <Message Text="============================================" Importance="high" />
  </Target>

</Project>
```

  <!-- Package mod to SLP mods folder for testing as released mod -->

    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)descriptions.json" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)stationpediaascended_ui" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\About.xml" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)\About" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\Preview.png" DestinationFolder="$(ModsFolder)\About" />

    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)descriptions.json" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)stationpediaascended_ui" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\About.xml" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)\About" />
    <Copy SourceFiles="$(ProjectDir)About\Preview.png" DestinationFolder="$(LocalModsFolder)\About" />
    
    <!-- Copy images folder using Exec -->
    <Exec Command="xcopy /Y /E /I &quot;$(ProjectDir)images&quot; &quot;$(ModsFolder)\images&quot;" />
    <Exec Command="xcopy /Y /E /I &quot;$(ProjectDir)images&quot; &quot;$(LocalModsFolder)\images&quot;" />

    <Message Text="============================================" Importance="high" />
  </Target>

</Project>
```

---

_**User (2026-01-18 10:09:14Z)**_

---

Let me look at the SetPageLore postfix to see if we need to filter out mechanics: 

The SetPageLore postfix just modifies the layout. Let me look at the SetPageGuides postfix and where the buttons are created: 

I see the problem now. In the `SetPageLore_Postfix` (Universe page), we're only calling `ModifyGuideLoreLayout` but we're not removing mechanics from the content. The mechanics are likely being shown because the game's SetPageLore method adds all registered pages to LoreGuideContents.

The fix is to filter out mechanics from the Universe/Lore page in the postfix. Let me add that:

Let me look at how the list items in LoreGuideContents are structured to find the right component: 

I see that mechanics are being added to `LoreGuideContents`. Let me check where the SetPageLore patch was added and understand the compile error better. Let me look at what was added: 

I see the issue - the code is using `StationpediaLinkHandler` which doesn't exist. Looking at how mechanics are added to the lore page (in the code around line 3663), I can see that buttons are created using `stationpedia.ListSearchPrefab` with an `onClick` listener. 

The problem is that we're not storing any identifier on the button for which page it links to. Let me look at how we can identify which children are mechanics. Since we added them ourselves, we can look at how they're named or check their onClick targets. But the simplest approach would be to **not add mechanics to LoreGuideContents in the first place**.

I see. The `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` correctly adds mechanics ONLY when navigating to the Game Mechanics page (line 3611 sets the title to "Game Mechanics"). The problem is that something is also adding mechanics to the Lore/Universe page. Let me look at where SetPageLore is called and how it's patched: 

I see! Now I understand the structure better. The `SetPageLore()` method uses `PopulateGuideLoreContents(LorePages, important: false)` where `LorePages` is a static list of keys. The mechanics are somehow being added to `LorePages`.

Let me search for where mechanics pages might be registered as Lore: 

Now let me check where mechanics pages are being registered: 

I found the issue! In `RegisterMechanicsPages()`, when creating the page, the `DisplayFilter` property is NOT being set. By default, it's probably `SPDAEntryType.None` or `Lore` (value 0). Let me check the SPDAEntryType enum: 

Now I understand. When `DisplayFilter` is not set (defaults to `Undefined` = 0), it doesn't match Guides or Lore, so it shouldn't be added to LorePages. But let me double-check the Register method to be sure: 

Okay, so if `DisplayFilter == SPDAEntryType.Lore`, it adds to `LorePages`. Since our mechanics pages are not setting `DisplayFilter`, they shouldn't be added to `LorePages`. But wait, perhaps they're being added somewhere else.

Let me look more carefully at the whole issue. Maybe our code is also adding mechanics to the lore page directly?

Let me check if there's a CreateGameMechanicsButton function that might be adding them: 

Let me look at the HomePageLayoutManager to understand how buttons are placed: 

The structure is clear. So when clicking the Universe button it calls `SetPageLore()`. But the mechanics shouldn't be there because they're not registered with `DisplayFilter = SPDAEntryType.Lore`. Let me trace this more carefully.

Let me check where mechanics pages might be getting registered with Lore: 

Let me check SurvivalManualLoader for Lore registration: 

No `DisplayFilter` here either. Let me check if any of the JSON guides are being registered with Lore: 

None of them are setting `DisplayFilter = SPDAEntryType.Lore`. So the mechanics shouldn't be going into LorePages. 

Let me look more carefully at the error. The compile error says `StationpediaLinkHandler` doesn't exist. That's the issue - I added code that tried to use a non-existent class. Let me fix the code properly.

Since we're the ones adding mechanics buttons to `LoreGuideContents` in `PopulateGameMechanicsContents()`, and the code is **also** called from `SetPageLore` via its postfix patch, I need to understand the call flow better.

Let me check the actual problem: Does `SetPageLore` somehow share the same container as our `SetPageGameMechanics`?

Looking back at line 3663, mechanics are added to `LoreGuideContents` in `PopulateGameMechanicsContents`. And `SetPageLore` also uses `LoreGuideContents` via `PopulateGuideLoreContents(LorePages, important: false)`.

The issue is that `LoreGuideContents` is being reused between different pages! So when we call `SetPageGameMechanics`, the mechanics are added to `LoreGuideContents`. Then when the user clicks Universe, `SetPageLore` is called which calls `PopulateGuideLoreContents` which SHOULD clear and repopulate... Let me check if that's the case: 

I see! The `PopulateGuideLoreContents` only destroys items in `_SPDAGuideLoreInserts`. But our `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` adds items directly to `LoreGuideContents` WITHOUT adding them to `_SPDAGuideLoreInserts`. So when `SetPageLore` runs, it clears `_SPDAGuideLoreInserts`, but our mechanics items remain in the container!

Now I understand the bug! The fix should be in `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` - we need to actually clear ALL children from `LoreGuideContents`, not just assume the vanilla code will do it, or we need to store our items in `_SPDAGuideLoreInserts` too.

Looking at our code at lines 3634-3638, we DO clear everything:

```csharp
foreach (Transform child in stationpedia.LoreGuideContents)
{
    UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(child.gameObject);
}
```

But the problem is the REVERSE - when vanilla `SetPageLore` is called after our `SetPageGameMechanics`, vanilla code only destroys items in `_SPDAGuideLoreInserts`, leaving OUR mechanics buttons behind!

The fix should be to also clear all children from `LoreGuideContents` at the start of `SetPageLore` postfix (or better, a prefix). Let me fix the compile error and implement a proper solution:

        /// <summary>
        /// Postfix patch for SetPageLore - modifies button layout to two columns and removes mechanics
        /// </summary>
        public static void Stationpedia_SetPageLore_Postfix(Stationpedia __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                // Remove any mechanics that may have been added to the Lore page
                // (Mechanics should only appear on the Game Mechanics page, not Universe)
                RemoveMechanicsFromLorePage(__instance);
                
                HomePageLayoutManager.ModifyGuideLoreLayout(__instance.LoreGuideContents);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error modifying lore layout: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Remove any Game Mechanics entries from the Lore (Universe) page
        /// </summary>
        private static void RemoveMechanicsFromLorePage(Stationpedia stationpedia)
        {
            if (stationpedia?.LoreGuideContents == null) return;
            
            var mechanicKeys = Data.GameMechanicsRegistry.GameMechanicsPages;
            if (mechanicKeys == null || mechanicKeys.Count == 0) return;
            
            // Collect children to remove (can't modify collection while iterating)
            var toRemove = new List<GameObject>();
            
            foreach (Transform child in stationpedia.LoreGuideContents)
            {
                // Check if this is a list item for a mechanic page
                var linkHandler = child.GetComponent<StationpediaLinkHandler>();
                if (linkHandler != null && mechanicKeys.Contains(linkHandler.PageLink))
                {
                    toRemove.Add(child.gameObject);
                }
            }
            
            // Remove the mechanic entries
            foreach (var obj in toRemove)
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(obj);
            }
            
            if (toRemove.Count > 0)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Removed {toRemove.Count} mechanics from Universe page");
            }
        }
```

            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error modifying lore layout: {ex.Message}");

- newString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Prefix patch for SetPageLore - clear orphaned items before vanilla repopulates
        /// When switching from Game Mechanics to Universe, our items remain because vanilla
        /// only clears items it tracks in _SPDAGuideLoreInserts. This clears everything first.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Stationpedia_SetPageLore_Prefix(Stationpedia __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                if (__instance?.LoreGuideContents == null) return;
                
                // Clear ALL children from LoreGuideContents before vanilla repopulates
                // This ensures our Game Mechanics buttons don't persist when switching to Universe
                var toRemove = new List<GameObject>();
                foreach (Transform child in __instance.LoreGuideContents)
                {
                    toRemove.Add(child.gameObject);
                }
                
                foreach (var obj in toRemove)
                {
                    UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(obj);
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error in SetPageLore prefix: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Postfix patch for SetPageLore - modifies button layout to two columns
        /// </summary>
        public static void Stationpedia_SetPageLore_Postfix(Stationpedia __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                HomePageLayoutManager.ModifyGuideLoreLayout(__instance.LoreGuideContents);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error modifying lore layout: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Postfix patch for SetPageLore - modifies button layout to two columns and removes mechanics
        /// </summary>
        public static void Stationpedia_SetPageLore_Postfix(Stationpedia __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                // Remove any mechanics that may have been added to the Lore page
                // (Mechanics should only appear on the Game Mechanics page, not Universe)
                RemoveMechanicsFromLorePage(__instance);
                
                HomePageLayoutManager.ModifyGuideLoreLayout(__instance.LoreGuideContents);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error modifying lore layout: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Remove any Game Mechanics entries from the Lore (Universe) page
        /// </summary>
        private static void RemoveMechanicsFromLorePage(Stationpedia stationpedia)
        {
            if (stationpedia?.LoreGuideContents == null) return;
            
            var mechanicKeys = Data.GameMechanicsRegistry.GameMechanicsPages;
            if (mechanicKeys == null || mechanicKeys.Count == 0) return;
            
            // Collect children to remove (can't modify collection while iterating)
            var toRemove = new List<GameObject>();
            
            foreach (Transform child in stationpedia.LoreGuideContents)
            {
                // Check if this is a list item for a mechanic page
                var linkHandler = child.GetComponent<StationpediaLinkHandler>();
                if (linkHandler != null && mechanicKeys.Contains(linkHandler.PageLink))
                {
                    toRemove.Add(child.gameObject);
                }
            }
            
            // Remove the mechanic entries
            foreach (var obj in toRemove)
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(obj);
            }
            
            if (toRemove.Count > 0)
            {
                ConsoleWindow.Print($"[Stationpedia Ascended] Removed {toRemove.Count} mechanics from Universe page");
            }
        }
```

                // Welcome file with formatting examples
                globalFolder.Files.Add(new PlannerFile
                {
                    Id = Guid.NewGuid().ToString(),
                    Name = "Welcome to Station Notepad",
                    Content = "{HEADER:Thank You for Subscribing!}\n\n" +
                             "{COLOR=#FF7A18}Thank you for using {B}Stationpedia Ascended{/B}!{/COLOR}\n\n" +
                             "We hope this mod enhances your Stationeers experience. " +
                             "This notepad is your personal space for notes, recipes, and plans.\n\n" +
                             "{COLOR=#888888}Feel free to delete this note once you've read it.{/COLOR}\n\n" +
                             "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n" +
                             "{HEADER:How to Use Station Notepad}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Creating Notes & Folders{/B}\n" +
                             "• Click {B}+ Note{/B} to create a new note\n" +
                             "• Click {B}+ Folder{/B} to create a new folder\n" +
                             "• {B}Right-click{/B} items to rename, delete, or move them\n" +
                             "• Drag and drop to reorganize\n\n" +
                             "{B}Saving{/B}\n" +
                             "• Notes auto-save when you switch files or close the window\n" +
                             "• Click the {B}Save{/B} button to manually save\n\n" +
                             "{B}Editor Modes{/B}\n" +
                             "• {B}Rich Text{/B} - Type formatting codes directly\n" +
                             "• {B}Block Editor{/B} - Visual editing with toolbar buttons\n\n" +
                             "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n" +
                             "{HEADER:Text Formatting}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Bold{/B}\n" +
                             "Type: \\{B\\}your text\\{/B\\}\n" +
                             "Result: {B}your text{/B}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Italic{/B}\n" +
                             "Type: \\{I\\}your text\\{/I\\}\n" +
                             "Result: {I}your text{/I}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Bold + Italic{/B}\n" +
                             "Type: \\{B\\}\\{I\\}your text\\{/I\\}\\{/B\\}\n" +
                             "Result: {B}{I}your text{/I}{/B}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Colored Text{/B}\n" +
                             "Type: \\{COLOR=#FF7A18\\}orange text\\{/COLOR\\}\n" +
                             "Result: {COLOR=#FF7A18}orange text{/COLOR}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Common Colors:{/B}\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#FF0000}Red{/COLOR} = #FF0000\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#00FF00}Green{/COLOR} = #00FF00\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#0088FF}Blue{/COLOR} = #0088FF\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#FF7A18}Orange{/COLOR} = #FF7A18\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#FFD700}Gold{/COLOR} = #FFD700\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#888888}Gray{/COLOR} = #888888\n\n" +
                             "{B}Headers{/B}\n" +
                             "Type: \\{HEADER:Section Title\\}\n" +
                             "Result: {HEADER:Section Title}\n\n" +
                             "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n" +
                             "{HEADER:Stationpedia Links}\n\n" +
                             "Create clickable links that open Stationpedia pages!\n\n" +
                             "{B}Syntax:{/B} \\{LINK:PageKey;Display Text\\}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Examples:{/B}\n" +
                             "• {LINK:StructureArcFurnace;Arc Furnace} - Smelting recipes\n" +
                             "• {LINK:ItemSteelIngot;Steel Ingot} - How to make steel\n" +
                             "• {LINK:StructureSolarPanel;Solar Panel} - Power generation\n" +
                             "• {LINK:ItemGasFilterCarbonDioxide;CO2 Filter} - Atmosphere control\n\n" +
                             "{COLOR=#888888}Tip: Find PageKeys by searching in Stationpedia!{/COLOR}\n\n" +
                             "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n" +
                             "{HEADER:Folder Types}\n\n" +
                             "{B}Global Notes{/B} {COLOR=#00FF00}(Green){/COLOR}\n" +
                             "• Visible in {I}all{/I} save files\n" +
                             "• Great for recipes, reference info, general tips\n\n" +
                             "{B}Per-Save Folders{/B} {COLOR=#0088FF}(Blue){/COLOR}\n" +
                             "• Only visible in that specific save\n" +
                             "• Perfect for base plans, to-do lists, world-specific notes\n" +
                             "• Created automatically or via right-click menu\n\n" +
                             "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n" +
                             "{HEADER:Keyboard Shortcuts}\n\n" +
                             "• {B}F2{/B} - Toggle notepad window\n" +
                             "• {B}Escape{/B} - Close window\n" +
                             "• {B}Ctrl+S{/B} - Save current note\n\n" +
                             "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n" +
                             "{COLOR=#FF7A18}{B}Happy note-taking, Stationeer!{/B}{/COLOR}\n" +
                             "{COLOR=#888888}Report bugs or suggestions on the Steam Workshop page.{/COLOR}"
                });

The issue is that `LoadFileSystem()` is called and selects the file, but the block editor might not be initialized yet at that point. Let me check when `LoadFileSystem` is called relative to `_blockEditor` initialization: 

Now let me check when the block editor is created: 

So `_blockEditor` is created during `CreateWindow()`, which is called BEFORE `LoadFileSystem()`. The issue might be that `Initialize()` runs async or the `IsInitialized` property returns false initially.

Let me check the BlockEditor initialization: 

The `IsInitialized` check is `_inputField != null`. Since `Initialize` is called synchronously before `LoadFileSystem`, this should be fine.

Let me look at how the welcome content is created - maybe there's an issue with special characters in the content: 

The content looks fine. The issue is that `SelectFile` is looking for JSON format (starting with `{`) but our content starts with `{HEADER:...}` which also starts with `{`. Let me check the JSON detection logic: 

The problem is that our content starts with `{HEADER:...}` which is being mistakenly detected as JSON because it starts with `{`. The `DeserializeDocument` call is likely failing silently.

Let me check the DeserializeDocument method to see how it handles invalid JSON: 

I see! When JSON parsing fails, it sets the content to empty string `""`. But our welcome content starts with `{HEADER:...}` which looks like JSON but isn't valid JSON, so it fails and shows nothing.

The fix should be to improve the JSON detection - check for `{"` instead of just `{`:

- newString:

```
            _currentFolder = null; // Deselect folder when file is selected
            
            if (_blockEditor != null && _blockEditor.IsInitialized && file != null)
            {
                // Try to load as block editor format first, fall back to plain text
                // Check for JSON object start ({"lines":...) not just any { which could be formatting tags like {HEADER:...}
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(file.Content) && file.Content.TrimStart().StartsWith("{\""))
                {
                    // Looks like JSON - try to load as block editor format
                    _blockEditor.DeserializeDocument(file.Content);
                }
                else
                {
                    // Plain text - load with all Normal styles
                    _blockEditor.LoadFromPlainText(file.Content ?? "");
                }
                
                _blockEditor.InputField.ActivateInputField();
            }
```

                // Welcome file with formatting examples
                globalFolder.Files.Add(new PlannerFile
                {
                    Id = Guid.NewGuid().ToString(),
                    Name = "Welcome to Station Notepad",
                    Content = "THANK YOU FOR SUBSCRIBING!\n" +
                             "=========================\n\n" +
                             "Thank you for using Stationpedia Ascended!\n\n" +
                             "This notepad is your personal space for notes,\n" +
                             "recipes, and plans. Feel free to delete this\n" +
                             "note once you've read it.\n\n" +
                             "-------------------------------------------\n\n" +
                             "HOW TO USE STATION NOTEPAD\n" +
                             "--------------------------\n\n" +
                             "Creating Notes & Folders:\n" +
                             "  * Click NOTE to create a new note\n" +
                             "  * Click FOLDER to create a new folder\n" +
                             "  * Right-click items to rename or delete\n" +
                             "  * Drag and drop to reorganize\n\n" +
                             "Saving:\n" +
                             "  * Notes auto-save when you switch files\n" +
                             "  * Notes auto-save when you close the window\n\n" +
                             "-------------------------------------------\n\n" +
                             "FOLDER TYPES\n" +
                             "------------\n\n" +
                             "Global Notes (marked with G):\n" +
                             "  * Visible in ALL save files\n" +
                             "  * Great for recipes and reference info\n\n" +
                             "Per-Save Folders:\n" +
                             "  * Only visible in that specific save\n" +
                             "  * Perfect for base plans and to-do lists\n" +
                             "  * Right-click a folder to make it per-save\n\n" +
                             "-------------------------------------------\n\n" +
                             "KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS\n" +
                             "------------------\n\n" +
                             "  F2      - Toggle notepad window\n" +
                             "  Escape  - Close window\n\n" +
                             "-------------------------------------------\n\n" +
                             "Happy note-taking, Stationeer!\n\n" +
                             "Report bugs or suggestions on the\n" +
                             "Steam Workshop page."
                });
```

                             "• {COLOR=#00FF00}Green{/COLOR} = #00FF00\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#0088FF}Blue{/COLOR} = #0088FF\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#FF7A18}Orange{/COLOR} = #FF7A18\n" +
                             "• {COLOR=#FFD700}Gold{/COLOR} = #FFD700\n" +

                             "{COLOR=#FF7A18}{B}Happy note-taking, Stationeer!{/B}{/COLOR}\n" +

- `{B}Creating Notes & Folders{/B}` is showing as raw text
- The horizontal lines are showing as boxes (corrupted Unicode characters)

The notepad is using the BlockEditor which apparently doesn't support these formatting tags - it's a plain text editor. The formatting tags like `{HEADER:...}`, `{B}...{/B}`, `{COLOR=...}` are probably meant for a different rendering system (like the Stationpedia page rendering), not the notepad's BlockEditor.

So I need to simplify the welcome note to just be plain text without any formatting tags since the notepad doesn't support rich text rendering in the way I was trying to use it.

_**User (2026-01-18 16:58:39Z)**_

MASSIVE bugs in the sationpedia ascended mod. Right now the sarch function in the stationpedia si completely broken and the text is not deserializing properly in the notepad. 

When you search the items are only appearing for a second then they disappear. We broke something with search that is fundamental.

Also the notepad hotkey to open it should be the f4 key not f2 since f2 is for the helper tips


---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Let me investigate these critical bugs: 

Let me check the SetPageLore prefix we added - it might be causing the issue: 

Now let me check the F2 hotkey in the notepad: 

Found the issues! F2 is used by the game for `ToggleHelperHints`. Let me check what the search issue could be - looking at the search container specifically: 

Now let me look at the game's Stationpedia source to understand the search containers better: 

Good - `SearchContents` and `LoreGuideContents` are DIFFERENT RectTransforms, so the SetPageLore prefix shouldn't affect search. Let me look at what else could be clearing search results. Let me check if there's an issue with the ClearPreviousSearch being called: 

I notice that the ClearPreviousSearch patch is registered TWICE - at lines 1339 and 1757. This could be causing the double-patching issue.

Let me also check if there's something in the SearchPatches that might be hiding search results: 

I see the problem! In the `ReorganizeSearchResults` function, when `FindPageByTitle` returns null (which can happen for various reasons), the code hides that item (`itemsToHide.Add(child)`). This is aggressive - if the index isn't working correctly, it could hide all search results!

Let me also check the issue more closely. The key line is:
```csharp
else
{
    // Hide orphan items since they may be debris/junk
    itemsToHide.Add(child);
}
```

This is hiding items that can't be matched to a page. If the `_pageTitleIndex` isn't built properly or if there's a title mismatch, all items get hidden.

Now let me fix all three issues:

1. **Change F2 to F4** for the notepad hotkey
2. **Fix the search issue** - don't hide items when page can't be found
3. **Check the notepad deserialization** 

Now let me also update the welcome note to mention F4 instead of F2: 

Now let me rebuild and test:
</details></think>

This is properly escaped JSON inside a JSON string. When Newtonsoft deserializes this, the `file.Content` property will contain:
```

  "lines": [

      "text": "2",

    }
  ]
}
```

- newString:

```
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Deserialize and load a document from JSON.
        /// </summary>
        public void DeserializeDocument(string json)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(json))
            {
                SetContent("", new List<LineStyle>());
                return;
            }
            
            try
            {
                var doc = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<BlockEditorDocument>(json);
                
                // Check if deserialization actually worked
                if (doc == null || doc.lines == null || doc.lines.Count == 0)
                {
                    // Failed to parse as block editor format, load as plain text
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning($"[BlockEditor] JSON parsed but no lines found, loading as plain text");
                    LoadFromPlainText(json);
                    return;
                }
                
                var text = new StringBuilder();
                var styles = new List<LineStyle>();
                
                for (int i = 0; i < doc.lines.Count; i++)
                {
                    if (i > 0) text.Append('\n');
                    text.Append(doc.lines[i].text);
                    styles.Add((LineStyle)doc.lines[i].style);
                }
                
                SetContent(text.ToString(), styles);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError($"[BlockEditor] Failed to deserialize document: {ex.Message}");
                // Fall back to loading as plain text instead of empty
                LoadFromPlainText(json);
            }
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Deserialize and load a document from JSON.
        /// </summary>
        public void DeserializeDocument(string json)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(json))
            {
                SetContent("", new List<LineStyle>());
                return;
            }
            
            try
            {
                var doc = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<BlockEditorDocument>(json);
                
                var text = new StringBuilder();
                var styles = new List<LineStyle>();
                
                for (int i = 0; i < doc.lines.Count; i++)
                {
                    if (i > 0) text.Append('\n');
                    text.Append(doc.lines[i].text);
                    styles.Add((LineStyle)doc.lines[i].style);
                }
                
                SetContent(text.ToString(), styles);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError($"[BlockEditor] Failed to deserialize document: {ex.Message}");
                SetContent("", new List<LineStyle>());
            }
        }
```

_**User (2026-01-19 00:54:42Z)**_

When I have a header formatted in the station notepad and then next line try to make a bullet point the bullet point gets placed in the line that has the header and you can't make new bullet points properly. This is a bug with the station notepad in the stationpedia ascended mod

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Let me investigate the bullet point handling in the BlockEditor to understand this bug. 

Let me read the full BlockEditor.cs to understand the structure and find the styling methods: 

Now I need to understand the issue better. When the user types on a line with a header and presses Enter to make a new line, and then tries to apply a bullet style, the issue might be in how:
1. New lines are being handled when the previous line has a style
2. The line styles array is being synchronized when adding new lines

Let me also look at how the toolbar buttons trigger the bullet styling: 

I see! The issue is that the bullet button uses `InsertText("• ")` instead of `ToggleStyleOnCurrentLine(LineStyle.Bullet)`. This means when you click the bullet button, it just inserts a bullet character at the caret position - which could be anywhere, including at the end of a header line if the caret is there.

Let me look at how the header styling works and find the toolbar setup: 

I can see the issue! Looking at the code:

- **H1, H2, H3** use `ApplyLineStyle(LineStyle.H1/H2/H3)` - which applies proper line-level styling
- **Bullet** uses `InsertText("• ")` - which just inserts a bullet character wherever the caret is!

This is the root cause. The bullet button should use `ApplyLineStyle(LineStyle.Bullet)` like the header buttons do, not `InsertText()`. When the caret is at the end of a header line (after you just typed it), clicking bullet inserts the bullet character on that same line instead of moving to a new line and applying the bullet style.

Let me fix this: 

Now let me also check if there's a checkbox button that has the same issue: 

Good, no checkbox button in the toolbar. Let me build and test:
</details></think>

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Gets the current line index where the caret is positioned.
        /// Uses TMP's textInfo for accurate line detection in rich text.
        /// </summary>
        public int CurrentLineIndex
        {
            get
            {
                if (_inputField == null || _inputField.textComponent == null)
                    return 0;
                
                // Force TMP to update its text info
                _inputField.textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
                var textInfo = _inputField.textComponent.textInfo;
                
                if (textInfo == null || textInfo.characterCount == 0)
                    return 0;
                
                // Get caret position in the rich text
                int caretPos = _inputField.caretPosition;
                
                // Clamp to valid range
                if (caretPos >= textInfo.characterCount)
                    caretPos = textInfo.characterCount - 1;
                if (caretPos < 0)
                    caretPos = 0;
                
                // Use TMP's textInfo to find which line this character is on
                for (int lineIdx = 0; lineIdx < textInfo.lineCount; lineIdx++)
                {
                    var lineInfo = textInfo.lineInfo[lineIdx];
                    if (caretPos >= lineInfo.firstCharacterIndex && caretPos <= lineInfo.lastCharacterIndex)
                    {
                        return lineIdx;
                    }
                }
                
                // If caret is past all lines (e.g., at very end), return last line
                return Mathf.Max(0, textInfo.lineCount - 1);
            }
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Gets the current line index where the caret is positioned.
        /// </summary>
        public int CurrentLineIndex
        {
            get
            {
                if (_inputField == null || _inputField.textComponent == null)
                    return 0;
                    
                string text = GetPlainText();
                int caretPos = Mathf.Min(_inputField.caretPosition, text.Length);
                
                // Count newlines before caret position
                int lineIndex = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < caretPos; i++)
                {
                    if (text[i] == '\n')
                        lineIndex++;
                }
                
                return lineIndex;
            }
        }
```

Looking at `RefreshDisplay()`:
```csharp
public void RefreshDisplay()
{
    if (_inputField == null) return;
    
    _isUpdatingDisplay = true;
    
    try
    {
        // Save caret position
        int caretPos = _inputField.caretPosition;
        
        // Generate rich text from plain text + styles
        string richText = GenerateRichText();
        
        // Update the input field
        _inputField.text = richText;
        
        // Restore caret position (may need adjustment for added tags)
        _inputField.caretPosition = Mathf.Min(caretPos, richText.Length);
    }
    finally
    {
        _isUpdatingDisplay = false;
    }
}
```

The `StripRichTextTags` method handles removing formatting markup from user input:
```csharp
private string StripRichTextTags(string text)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(text)) return text;
    
    var result = new StringBuilder();
    bool inTag = false;
    
    foreach (char c in text)
    {
        if (c == '<')
        {
            inTag = true;
        }
        else if (c == '>')
        {
            inTag = false;
        }
        else if (!inTag)
        {
            result.Append(c);
        }
    }
    
    return result.ToString();
}
```

This strips HTML and TextMeshPro tags like `<color>` and `<size>` by skipping any characters between angle brackets. Importantly, it preserves non-tag content like bullet points and whitespace, so when the display shows `<color=#FBB03B>•</color> Hello`, the plain text extraction keeps the bullet and spaces intact.

When the user edits and we call `StripRichTextTags`, we get `• Hello` with the bullet character still there. Then `RefreshDisplay()` re-renders it with the BULLET_PREFIX constant (`  <color=#FBB03B>•</color> `), which becomes `  • ` after stripping. This creates duplicate bullets—the original one from the stripped text plus the new prefix.

The core issue is that the bullet character itself needs to be removed during plain text extraction, not just the formatting tags around it. I should reconsider the approach: when a user clicks the bullet button, it should apply the prefix to the line, but the extraction logic needs to account for removing both the tags and the actual bullet characters that result from stripping.

- newString:

```
        // Visual prefix that appears after tag stripping for bullet lines: "  • " (2 spaces, bullet, space)
        private const string BULLET_VISUAL_PREFIX = "  • ";
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Called when the input field text changes.
        /// Handles the downgrade-on-edit logic.
        /// </summary>
        private void OnTextChanged(string newText)
        {
            if (_isUpdatingDisplay) return;
            
            try
            {
                // Strip any rich text tags the user might have typed/pasted
                string plainText = StripRichTextTags(newText);
                
                // Split into lines
                string[] newLines = plainText.Split('\n');
                string[] oldLines = _previousPlainText.Split('\n');
                
                // Ensure we have enough line styles
                while (_lineStyles.Count < newLines.Length)
                {
                    _lineStyles.Add(LineStyle.Normal);
                }
                
                // Trim excess line styles
                while (_lineStyles.Count > newLines.Length)
                {
                    _lineStyles.RemoveAt(_lineStyles.Count - 1);
                }
                
                // Strip visual prefixes from styled lines (the prefix characters that remain after tag stripping)
                for (int i = 0; i < newLines.Length; i++)
                {
                    if (i < _lineStyles.Count && _lineStyles[i] == LineStyle.Bullet)
                    {
                        // Remove the visual bullet prefix if present
                        if (newLines[i].StartsWith(BULLET_VISUAL_PREFIX))
                        {
                            newLines[i] = newLines[i].Substring(BULLET_VISUAL_PREFIX.Length);
                        }
                        // Also handle case where user deleted some of the prefix
                        else if (newLines[i].StartsWith("  •"))
                        {
                            newLines[i] = newLines[i].TrimStart(' ', '•');
                        }
                        else if (newLines[i].StartsWith("•"))
                        {
                            newLines[i] = newLines[i].TrimStart('•', ' ');
                        }
                    }
                }
                
                // Rebuild plain text after stripping prefixes
                plainText = string.Join("\n", newLines);
                
                // Re-split for comparison (in case we modified lines)
                newLines = plainText.Split('\n');
                
                // Detect which lines were edited and downgrade them
                for (int i = 0; i < newLines.Length; i++)
                {
                    // Check if this line existed before and what it contained
                    bool wasEmpty = (i >= oldLines.Length || string.IsNullOrEmpty(oldLines[i]));
                    bool isNowEmpty = string.IsNullOrEmpty(newLines[i]);
                    bool contentChanged = (i >= oldLines.Length || newLines[i] != oldLines[i]);
                    
                    // Only downgrade if:
                    // 1. Content actually changed AND
                    // 2. The line wasn't empty before (preserve formatting on empty->text transitions)
                    if (contentChanged && !wasEmpty && !isNowEmpty)
                    {
                        // Only downgrade if the line has a non-normal style
                        if (_lineStyles[i] != LineStyle.Normal)
                        {
                            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"[BlockEditor] Line {i} edited, downgrading from {_lineStyles[i]} to Normal");
                            _lineStyles[i] = LineStyle.Normal;
                        }
                    }
                }
                
                // Update tracking
                _previousPlainText = plainText;
                _previousLineCount = newLines.Length;
                
                // Refresh display with proper formatting
                RefreshDisplay();
                
                // Trigger content changed event for auto-save
                OnContentChanged?.Invoke();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError($"[BlockEditor] OnTextChanged error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
```

        /// <summary>

        private void OnTextChanged(string newText)

                string plainText = StripRichTextTags(newText);
                
                // Split into lines
                string[] newLines = plainText.Split('\n');
                string[] oldLines = _previousPlainText.Split('\n');
                
                // Ensure we have enough line styles
                while (_lineStyles.Count < newLines.Length)

                }

                }

                for (int i = 0; i < newLines.Length; i++)

                    bool isNowEmpty = string.IsNullOrEmpty(newLines[i]);
                    bool contentChanged = (i >= oldLines.Length || newLines[i] != oldLines[i]);
                    
                    // Only downgrade if:

        {

                // Clamp caret position
                if (caretPos > displayText.Length)
                    caretPos = displayText.Length;

        /// Gets the current line index where the caret is positioned.
        /// Uses TMP's textInfo for accurate line detection in rich text.
        /// </summary>
        public int CurrentLineIndex
        {
            get
            {
                if (_inputField == null || _inputField.textComponent == null)
                    return 0;
                
                // Force TMP to update its text info
                _inputField.textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
                var textInfo = _inputField.textComponent.textInfo;
                
                if (textInfo == null || textInfo.characterCount == 0)
                    return 0;
                
                // Get caret position in the rich text
                int caretPos = _inputField.caretPosition;
                
                // Clamp to valid range
                if (caretPos >= textInfo.characterCount)
                    caretPos = textInfo.characterCount - 1;
                if (caretPos < 0)
                    caretPos = 0;
                
                // Use TMP's textInfo to find which line this character is on
                for (int lineIdx = 0; lineIdx < textInfo.lineCount; lineIdx++)
                {
                    var lineInfo = textInfo.lineInfo[lineIdx];
                    if (caretPos >= lineInfo.firstCharacterIndex && caretPos <= lineInfo.lastCharacterIndex)
                    {
                        return lineIdx;
                    }
                }
                
                // If caret is past all lines (e.g., at very end), return last line
                return Mathf.Max(0, textInfo.lineCount - 1);
            }

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Gets the current line index where the caret is positioned.
        /// Uses the parsed/rendered text (without tags) to correctly map caret position to line.
        /// </summary>
        public int CurrentLineIndex
        {
            get
            {
                if (_inputField == null || _inputField.textComponent == null)
                    return 0;
                
                // Force mesh update to ensure textInfo is current
                _inputField.textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
                
                // Get the parsed text (what's actually displayed, without rich text tags)
                string parsedText = _inputField.textComponent.GetParsedText();
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(parsedText))
                    return 0;
                
                int caretPos = _inputField.caretPosition;
                
                // Clamp caret position to parsed text length
                if (caretPos > parsedText.Length)
                    caretPos = parsedText.Length;
                if (caretPos < 0)
                    caretPos = 0;
                
                // Count newlines in the PARSED text up to caret position
                int lineIndex = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < caretPos; i++)
                {
                    if (parsedText[i] == '\n')
                        lineIndex++;
                }
                
                return lineIndex;
            }
        }
        
        #endregion
        
        #region Initialization
```

        /// <summary>
        /// Apply a style to a specific line.
        /// </summary>
        public void ApplyStyleToLine(int lineIndex, LineStyle style)
        {
            // Ensure we have enough styles
            while (_lineStyles.Count <= lineIndex)
            {
                _lineStyles.Add(LineStyle.Normal);
            }
            
            _lineStyles[lineIndex] = style;
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"[BlockEditor] Applied {style} to line {lineIndex}");
            
            RefreshDisplay();
            
            // Position caret after the prefix for bullet style
            if (style == LineStyle.Bullet)
            {
                PositionCaretAfterPrefix(lineIndex);
            }
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Position the caret after the line's prefix (e.g., bullet character).
        /// </summary>
        private void PositionCaretAfterPrefix(int lineIndex)
        {
            if (_inputField == null || _inputField.textComponent == null) return;
            
            // Force update to get accurate parsed text
            _inputField.textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();
            string parsedText = _inputField.textComponent.GetParsedText();
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(parsedText)) return;
            
            // Find the start of the target line in the parsed text
            string[] parsedLines = parsedText.Split('\n');
            int charPos = 0;
            
            for (int i = 0; i < lineIndex && i < parsedLines.Length; i++)
            {
                charPos += parsedLines[i].Length + 1; // +1 for newline
            }
            
            // Now add the length of the bullet prefix ("• " = 2 chars)
            if (lineIndex < parsedLines.Length && parsedLines[lineIndex].StartsWith("• "))
            {
                charPos += 2; // Skip past "• "
            }
            
            // Set caret position
            _inputField.caretPosition = charPos;
            _inputField.selectionAnchorPosition = charPos;
            _inputField.selectionFocusPosition = charPos;
            
            // Ensure input field stays focused
            _inputField.ActivateInputField();
```

- oldString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Apply a style to a specific line.
        /// </summary>
        public void ApplyStyleToLine(int lineIndex, LineStyle style)
        {
            // Ensure we have enough styles
            while (_lineStyles.Count <= lineIndex)
            {
                _lineStyles.Add(LineStyle.Normal);
            }
            
            _lineStyles[lineIndex] = style;
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"[BlockEditor] Applied {style} to line {lineIndex}");
            
            RefreshDisplay();
            
            // Don't reposition cursor - let it stay where the user has it
            // The prefix will appear before the text but cursor remains in place
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Position the caret after the line's prefix (bullet/checkbox).
        /// </summary>
        private void PositionCaretAfterPrefix(int lineIndex)
        {
            if (_inputField == null) return;
            
            // Calculate the character position at the start of this line's content
            string[] lines = _previousPlainText.Split('\n');
            int charPos = 0;
            
            // Sum up lengths of all previous lines plus newlines
            for (int i = 0; i < lineIndex && i < lines.Length; i++)
            {
                charPos += lines[i].Length + 1; // +1 for newline
            }
            
            // Position at start of line content (after any visual prefix)
            _inputField.caretPosition = charPos;
            _inputField.selectionAnchorPosition = charPos;
```

        /// First row is treated as headers (bold), all cells are center-aligned.
        /// Uses nested section background styling.
        /// </summary>
        private static void CreateTableElement(RectTransform parent, TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI sourceText, List<TableRow> tableData)
        {
            try
            {
                if (tableData == null || tableData.Count == 0) return;
                
                // Determine column count from first row
                int columnCount = tableData[0].cells?.Count ?? 0;
                if (columnCount == 0) return;
                
                // Calculate equal column width percentage
                float columnWidthPercent = 1f / columnCount;
                
                // Create outer container with background (like nested sections)
                var tableContainerGO = new GameObject("TableContainer");
                tableContainerGO.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
                
                var containerRT = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
                containerRT.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
                containerRT.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);
                containerRT.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                
                // Add background image (like nested sections)
                var bgImage = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Image>();
                if (VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)
                {
                    if (_nativePanelSprite != null)
                    {
                        bgImage.sprite = _nativePanelSprite;
                        bgImage.type = _nativePanelType;
                        bgImage.material = _nativePanelMaterial;
                    }
                    bgImage.color = Color.white;
                }
                else
                {
                    var windowSprite = StationpediaAscendedMod.GetWindowBackgroundSprite();
                    if (windowSprite != null)
                    {
                        bgImage.sprite = windowSprite;
                        bgImage.type = UnityEngine.UI.Image.Type.Sliced;
                    }
                    bgImage.color = StationeersBlue;
                }
                
                // Add vertical layout for rows
                var containerLayout = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                containerLayout.padding = new RectOffset(12, 12, 10, 10);
                containerLayout.spacing = 4;
                containerLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                containerLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                containerLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                containerLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                
                // Auto-size the container
                var containerFitter = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                containerFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                containerFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                
                // Create each row
                for (int rowIndex = 0; rowIndex < tableData.Count; rowIndex++)
                {
                    var rowData = tableData[rowIndex];
                    if (rowData?.cells == null) continue;
                    
                    bool isHeader = (rowIndex == 0);
                    
                    // Create row container with explicit anchoring
                    var rowGO = new GameObject($"TableRow_{rowIndex}");
                    rowGO.transform.SetParent(tableContainerGO.transform, false);
                    
                    var rowRT = rowGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
                    rowRT.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
                    rowRT.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);
                    rowRT.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                    
                    // Add horizontal layout for cells - using LayoutGroup for equal distribution
                    var rowLayout = rowGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
                    rowLayout.spacing = 4;
                    rowLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;  // Force all children to expand equally
                    rowLayout.childForceExpandHeight = true;
                    rowLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                    rowLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                    rowLayout.childAlignment = UnityEngine.TextAnchor.MiddleCenter;
                    
                    // Add row size fitter
                    var rowFitter = rowGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    rowFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                    rowFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                    
                    // Create cells (ensure we have the right column count)
                    for (int colIndex = 0; colIndex < columnCount; colIndex++)
                    {
                        string cellText = colIndex < rowData.cells.Count ? rowData.cells[colIndex] : "";
                        
                        // Create cell container
                        var cellGO = new GameObject($"Cell_{rowIndex}_{colIndex}");
                        cellGO.transform.SetParent(rowGO.transform, false);
                        
                        var cellRT = cellGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
                        
                        // Add layout element - all cells get equal flex width for even distribution
                        var cellLayout = cellGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                        cellLayout.flexibleWidth = 1f;  // Equal weight for all columns
                        cellLayout.flexibleHeight = 1f;
                        cellLayout.minHeight = 20f;
                        
                        // Create text element
                        var cellTextComponent = cellGO.AddComponent<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>();
                        cellTextComponent.font = sourceText.font;
                        cellTextComponent.fontSharedMaterial = sourceText.fontSharedMaterial;
                        cellTextComponent.fontSize = sourceText.fontSize * 0.85f;
                        cellTextComponent.color = sourceText.color;
                        cellTextComponent.enableWordWrapping = true;
                        cellTextComponent.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Overflow;
                        cellTextComponent.alignment = TMPro.TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
                        cellTextComponent.richText = true;
                        cellTextComponent.margin = new Vector4(2, 2, 2, 2);
                        
                        // Header row is bold
                        if (isHeader)
                        {
                            string headerColor = VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode ? "#FFFFFF" : "#FFA500";
                            cellTextComponent.text = $"<b><color={headerColor}>{cellText}</color></b>";
```

        /// First row is treated as headers (bold), all cells are center-aligned.
        /// Uses nested section background styling.
        /// </summary>
        private static void CreateTableElement(RectTransform parent, TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI sourceText, List<TableRow> tableData)
        {
            try
            {
                if (tableData == null || tableData.Count == 0) return;
                
                // Determine column count from first row
                int columnCount = tableData[0].cells?.Count ?? 0;
                if (columnCount == 0) return;
                
                // Create outer container with background (like nested sections)
                var tableContainerGO = new GameObject("TableContainer");
                tableContainerGO.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
                
                var containerRT = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
                containerRT.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
                containerRT.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);
                containerRT.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);
                
                // Add background image (like nested sections)
                var bgImage = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Image>();
                if (VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)
                {
                    if (_nativePanelSprite != null)
                    {
                        bgImage.sprite = _nativePanelSprite;
                        bgImage.type = _nativePanelType;
                        bgImage.material = _nativePanelMaterial;
                    }
                    bgImage.color = Color.white;
                }
                else
                {
                    var windowSprite = StationpediaAscendedMod.GetWindowBackgroundSprite();
                    if (windowSprite != null)
                    {
                        bgImage.sprite = windowSprite;
                        bgImage.type = UnityEngine.UI.Image.Type.Sliced;
                    }
                    bgImage.color = StationeersBlue;
                }
                
                // Add vertical layout for rows
                var containerLayout = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.VerticalLayoutGroup>();
                containerLayout.padding = new RectOffset(12, 12, 10, 10);
                containerLayout.spacing = 4;
                containerLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                containerLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                containerLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                containerLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                
                // Auto-size the container
                var containerFitter = tableContainerGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                containerFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                containerFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                
                // Create each row
                for (int rowIndex = 0; rowIndex < tableData.Count; rowIndex++)
                {
                    var rowData = tableData[rowIndex];
                    if (rowData?.cells == null) continue;
                    
                    bool isHeader = (rowIndex == 0);
                    
                    // Create row container
                    var rowGO = new GameObject($"TableRow_{rowIndex}");
                    rowGO.transform.SetParent(tableContainerGO.transform, false);
                    
                    // Add horizontal layout for cells
                    var rowLayout = rowGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
                    rowLayout.spacing = 8;
                    rowLayout.childForceExpandWidth = true;
                    rowLayout.childForceExpandHeight = false;
                    rowLayout.childControlWidth = true;
                    rowLayout.childControlHeight = true;
                    rowLayout.childAlignment = UnityEngine.TextAnchor.MiddleCenter;
                    
                    // Add row size fitter
                    var rowFitter = rowGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter>();
                    rowFitter.horizontalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
                    rowFitter.verticalFit = UnityEngine.UI.ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
                    
                    // Create cells (ensure we have the right column count)
                    for (int colIndex = 0; colIndex < columnCount; colIndex++)
                    {
                        string cellText = colIndex < rowData.cells.Count ? rowData.cells[colIndex] : "";
                        
                        // Create cell container
                        var cellGO = new GameObject($"Cell_{rowIndex}_{colIndex}");
                        cellGO.transform.SetParent(rowGO.transform, false);
                        
                        // Add layout element for flexible width distribution
                        var cellLayout = cellGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
                        cellLayout.flexibleWidth = 1f;
                        cellLayout.minWidth = 50f;
                        
                        // Create text element
                        var cellTextComponent = cellGO.AddComponent<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>();
                        cellTextComponent.font = sourceText.font;
                        cellTextComponent.fontSharedMaterial = sourceText.fontSharedMaterial;
                        cellTextComponent.fontSize = sourceText.fontSize * 0.9f;
                        cellTextComponent.color = sourceText.color;
                        cellTextComponent.enableWordWrapping = true;
                        cellTextComponent.alignment = TMPro.TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
                        cellTextComponent.richText = true;
                        cellTextComponent.margin = new Vector4(4, 4, 4, 4);
                        
                        // Header row is bold
                        if (isHeader)
                        {
                            string headerColor = VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode ? "#FFFFFF" : "#FFA500";
                            cellTextComponent.text = $"<b><color={headerColor}>{cellText}</color></b>";
